articles.title: OpenAI just released its answer to Claude Mythos
articles.summary: OpenAI launched Daybreak, an AI security initiative using Codex Security to detect and patch code vulnerabilities before attackers exploit them. It analyzes code for threats and automates detection of high-risk vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Here’s what Mira Murati’s AI company is up to
articles.summary: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines announced 'interaction models' enabling real-time AI collaboration through audio, video, and text inputs, allowing continuous thinking and response capabilities.

articles.title: Google stopped a zero-day hack that it says was developed with AI
articles.summary: Google detected and stopped a zero-day exploit created with AI assistance that targeted an open-source system administration tool, preventing mass exploitation and two-factor authentication bypass attacks.

articles.title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
articles.summary: Robinhood files confidentially for a second venture fund targeting growth and early-stage startups, riding the current AI rally.

articles.title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
articles.summary: GM laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills. New positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering, cloud engineering, and prompt engineering.

articles.title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
articles.summary: Digg relaunches as an AI-powered news aggregator designed to identify influential voices and surface important news stories worth reading.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure and compete with AWS by addressing legacy cloud limitations.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing is facing competition from Goose, a free open-source AI coding agent by Block that offers similar functionality without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge helped recruit top engineering talent in a competitive hiring market.

articles.title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
articles.summary: Linux faces a second severe vulnerability in weeks called Dirty Frag, allowing low-privilege users and containers to gain root access. Exploit code leaked online, with evidence of active attacks in the wild.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.summary: A cyberattack disrupted Canvas learning platform during US finals, forcing schools offline. Instructure restored service Friday after identifying unauthorized network activity; user data including names and emails were accessed.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla's AI vulnerability detection tool Mythos identified 271 Firefox security flaws with minimal false positives. The breakthrough demonstrates AI's potential in software security, though skeptics remain cautious about overhyped AI claims.

articles.title: Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning economist
articles.summary: Nobel-winning economist Daron Acemoglu argues AI will provide modest productivity gains and won't eliminate human work, countering Silicon Valley's promises of workplace transformation.

articles.title: Fostering breakthrough AI innovation through customer-back engineering
articles.summary: Organizations waste digital investments by prioritizing technology first instead of customer needs. Successful AI implementation requires customer-back engineering, starting with customer problems and working backward to technology solutions.

articles.title: Innovation abounds in device charging
articles.summary: Device chargers have significantly evolved over the past decade, becoming smaller, safer, and faster through gallium nitride semiconductors, multi-port designs, and USB-C standardization, enabling single chargers to power multiple devices efficiently.

articles.title: Building Blocks for Foundation Model Training and Inference on AWS
articles.summary: AWS introduces new building blocks for training and deploying foundation models, providing infrastructure and tools to streamline large-scale AI model development and inference operations.

articles.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
articles.summary: EMO introduces a pretraining method for mixture of experts models that enables emergent modularity. The approach improves model specialization and efficiency by allowing different experts to develop specialized capabilities.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM updates from V0 to V1 prioritize correctness in reinforcement learning before making corrections. The framework ensures accuracy as foundational before implementing refinement processes.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and innovation. The conversation explores upcoming trends and opportunities in artificial intelligence advancement.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities in content creation and marketing initiatives.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft has open-sourced its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology tools available to the public for advancing sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: The new AI-powered Google Finance is expanding to Europe.
articles.summary: AI-powered Google Finance is launching across Europe with local language support, offering new powerful capabilities for financial management and insights.

articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launches The Small Brief, pairing renowned ad industry creative legends with local businesses to develop innovative AI-powered advertisements that champion and promote their work.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features offer gardening tips to help plants thrive. The article provides top ways to use these tools for gardening assistance.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve, powered by Gemini, is a coding agent scaling impact across business, infrastructure, and science through advanced algorithms.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing AI co-clinicians to augment healthcare delivery. This new model aims to enhance clinical care through AI-assisted decision-making and patient support.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with the Republic of Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman, claiming OpenAI was stolen from its charitable mission. Evidence revealed Musk may have used OpenAI models to train xAI.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: Episode 243 discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with coding improvements and safety features, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses major AI developments including OpenAI's improved ChatGPT image model for accurate text generation, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6, reflecting advances in multimodal AI capabilities.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing a critical challenge in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights via reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Google brings multi-token prediction Gemma 4 LLMs
articles.summary: Google's Gemma 4 LLMs introduce multi-token prediction technology, achieving 3-6x throughput improvements for local language models through community-driven optimization.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables large language models to process 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy, dramatically expanding context window capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code, an AI coding assistant, is inadvertently publishing sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries, creating security risks.

articles.title: Understanding LLM Distillation Techniques
articles.summary: LLM distillation involves using powerful teacher models to train smaller student models, reducing computational costs while maintaining performance. Major companies like Meta, Google, and DeepSeek use this technique to transfer reasoning and capabilities to efficient models.

articles.title: How to Build Technical Analysis and Backtesting Workflow with pandas-ta-classic, Strategy Signals, and Performance Metrics
articles.summary: Tutorial on building a complete technical analysis and trading strategy workflow using pandas-ta-classic, covering indicator calculation, signal generation, backtesting, and performance visualization.

articles.title: Meta and Stanford Researchers Propose Fast Byte Latent Transformer That Reduces Inference Memory Bandwidth by Over 50% Without Tokenization
articles.summary: Meta and Stanford researchers introduced Fast Byte Latent Transformer, which operates on raw bytes instead of tokens and reduces inference memory bandwidth by over 50% without requiring tokenization.

articles.title: Best Buy is selling this 4TB WD Black SSD for 65% off right now - and I'm seriously tempted
articles.summary: Best Buy is offering a 65% discount on the 4TB WD Black SN850X SSD, providing significant savings as PC component prices have risen.

articles.title: Windows 11's new Low Latency Profile may give your PC the speed boost it deserves
articles.summary: Windows 11's new Low Latency Profile, currently in early testing, aims to boost PC speed by improving app responsiveness, menus, and flyouts performance.

articles.title: Microsoft PowerToys now lets you control your monitor from the taskbar - here's how
articles.summary: Microsoft PowerToys adds monitor control to the taskbar, allowing users to adjust display settings directly from the system tray instead of using physical buttons or Windows settings.

articles.title: Implementing Prompt Compression to Reduce Agentic Loop Costs
articles.summary: Prompt compression techniques reduce costs in agentic loops by minimizing token usage in LLM calls and API requests. This addresses the expensive nature of production agentic systems where billing is heavily tied to token consumption.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: AI agents now implement permission-gated tool calling in Python, enabling controlled access to functions. This advancement allows agents to move beyond passive chatbots toward more autonomous and secure systems.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: The article provides guidance on implementing tool calling capabilities in AI agents, enabling them to effectively interact with external systems and APIs to accomplish complex tasks.

articles.title: Learning Word Vectors for Sentiment Analysis: A Python Reproduction
articles.summary: Guide on building sentiment-aware word vectors from IMDb reviews using semantic learning and linear SVM classification for sentiment analysis tasks.

articles.title: How to Build a Claude Code-Powered Knowledge Base
articles.summary: Learn to build a Claude-powered knowledge base for efficient personal data retrieval. The guide demonstrates creating intelligent systems using Claude's code capabilities.

articles.title: Using Transformers to Forecast Incredibly Rare Solar Flares
articles.summary: Machine learning transformers are being applied to predict rare solar flares, demonstrating how ML can be adapted for forecasting extremely infrequent events.

articles.title: ‘Your Career Starts at the Beginning of the AI Revolution,’ NVIDIA CEO Tells Graduates
articles.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang told Carnegie Mellon graduates they're entering an extraordinary moment at the beginning of the AI revolution with unprecedented opportunities to shape the future.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck argue that American AI leadership requires American energy leadership. They contend AI will help generate the energy needed to power the next century.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin Single Sign-On, enabling faster logins for gamers. Seven new games were added to the cloud gaming service this week.

articles.title: Building web search-enabled agents with Strands and Exa
articles.summary: Exa integrates with Strands Agents SDK to provide AI-native web search for agents, delivering structured content optimized for LLM workflows without requiring custom parsing or post-processing.

articles.title: Introducing Claude Platform on AWS: Anthropic’s native platform, through your AWS account
articles.summary: Anthropic launches Claude Platform on AWS, allowing customers to access Claude's native platform directly through their AWS accounts without separate credentials or billing. AWS becomes the first cloud provider offering this integrated experience.

articles.title: Manufacturing intelligence with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enables manufacturing organizations to search technical documents by mapping text, images, and diagrams into a shared vector space, allowing cross-modal queries across specifications and engineering visuals.

articles.title: Introducing NVIDIA Fleet Intelligence for Real-Time GPU Fleet Visibility and Optimization
articles.summary: NVIDIA introduces Fleet Intelligence for real-time GPU fleet visibility and optimization, addressing challenges of managing large-scale heterogeneous hardware, software stacks, power constraints, and multitenant workloads.

articles.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
articles.summary: Researchers improve Bash command generation in small language models using grammar-constrained decoding, enabling AI agents to produce valid executable shell commands for file operations and system interactions.

articles.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo adds support for streaming tokens and tools in multi-turn agentic interactions, enabling assistants to interleave reasoning with tool calls while preserving structured conversation flow.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examines how different generations perceive social media ads across platforms in collaboration with CrowdDNA, revealing generational differences in consumer attitudes toward advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon mitigation technology and sharing it openly to reduce its environmental footprint.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background leveraged their unique perspective to develop innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive technological breakthroughs in the XR field.

articles.title: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning: The Next Paradigm in Efficient Inference Scaling
articles.summary: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning enables AI models to independently decide when to parallelize subtasks, determine concurrent thread counts, and coordinate execution based on problem requirements, advancing efficient inference scaling.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, integrated stochasticity for exploration, and gradient reshaping for stable learning from high-dimensional vision.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify interactions at scale for Large Language Models, advancing interpretability by analyzing feature attribution and decision-making processes to create safer, more transparent AI systems.
